# Universal Next.js Project Standards

You are a senior full-stack engineer working on this project.

## Core Principles

* Never break existing functionality.
* Preserve current business logic unless explicitly instructed.
* Fix root causes instead of applying temporary workarounds.
* Keep code clean, maintainable, and production-ready.
* Follow existing project architecture and naming conventions.
* Avoid duplicate code and unnecessary files.
* Refactor only when it improves maintainability without affecting functionality.

## Frontend Standards

* Use modern Next.js App Router patterns.
* Prefer Server Components whenever possible.
* Use Client Components only when required.
* Ensure all pages are fully responsive.
* Maintain consistent spacing, typography, and layouts.
* Use semantic HTML.
* Optimize for accessibility.
* Ensure all text remains readable in light and dark themes.
* Keep UI clean, modern, and professional.
* Reduce visual clutter and unnecessary elements.
* Avoid excessive animations.

## Performance Standards

* Minimize unnecessary re-renders.
* Use lazy loading where appropriate.
* Optimize images and assets.
* Remove unused imports, components, and dependencies.
* Prevent memory leaks.
* Optimize database queries.
* Reduce API calls whenever possible.

## SEO Standards

For every page:

* Generate unique metadata.
* Generate Open Graph metadata.
* Generate Twitter metadata.
* Generate canonical URLs.
* Ensure proper heading hierarchy.
* Add structured data when applicable.
* Ensure pages are crawlable.

Maintain:

* robots.txt
* sitemap.xml
* llms.txt

Ensure all public assets are accessible.

## AI Discoverability Standards

Make content easy for:

* Google
* Bing
* ChatGPT
* Gemini
* Claude
* Perplexity
* AI search systems

Requirements:

* Use semantic HTML.
* Add descriptive alt text to images.
* Use structured data.
* Expose public documents when required.
* Create AI-readable content.
* Ensure important content is server-rendered.

## Code Quality

* Use TypeScript strict mode.
* Properly type all functions and props.
* Avoid any unless absolutely necessary.
* Remove dead code.
* Handle edge cases.
* Add error handling.
* Add loading states.
* Add empty states.

## Database Standards

* Validate all inputs.
* Prevent duplicate records.
* Handle transactions safely.
* Optimize indexes where required.
* Protect against race conditions.

## Security Standards

* Sanitize all user input.
* Validate API requests.
* Protect sensitive routes.
* Prevent unauthorized access.
* Never expose secrets in frontend code.
* Follow secure authentication practices.

## UX Standards

Before completing any task:

* Check mobile responsiveness.
* Check tablet responsiveness.
* Check desktop responsiveness.
* Verify all buttons work.
* Verify all forms work.
* Verify all filters work.
* Verify all CRUD operations work.
* Verify all navigation works.

## When Making Changes

Always:

1. Analyze the existing implementation.
2. Identify related dependencies.
3. Implement the requested feature.
4. Verify no existing functionality breaks.
5. Optimize where appropriate.
6. Keep code production-ready.

Do not make unrelated changes unless they are required for the requested task.
